texts.full_text: ief that the Eagle, unlike other birds, periodically renewed its plumage and its youth by flying near the sun then plunging into the water. The majestic Eagle was central to many mythologies and sacred writings of humanity. The ancient Greeks revered the eagle as a symbol of the god of lightning, and it is said they nailed Eagles to the peaks of temples to serve as magic lightning rods; Scandinavian myths also associate the Eagle with lightning and storm. The Hittites (an ancient people living in Anatolia and northern Syria about 2000-1200 B.C) used the double Eagle as an emblem of sovereignty. THE FESSE Represents a military belt or girdle of honour. The word Fesse is a French word; and signifies the loins of a man. The girdle of honour may seem to have been in ancient time given by Emperors, and Kings, and their Generals of the field unto soldiers, for reward of some special service performed by them. This Ordinary has been anciently taken for the same that we call Baltheum militare or a belt of honour. The bestowing of this military girdle was reputed very honourable because none were to receive it but men of merit. If a knight was disarmed of his Military girdle by his demerits and offence, he is there-with-all deprived of all Military privileges. texts.full_text: eld of the Invention The present invention relates to a plasma display panel, and more particularly, to a plasma display panel, in which a differential dielectric is formed on an upper plate to reduce breakdown voltage and discharge current, and a method for manufacturing the same. 2. Discussion of the Related Art Generally, in a plasma display panel, discharge cells are divided from each other by barrier ribs formed between a front substrate and a rear substrate. Each of the discharge cells is filled with a main discharge gas, such as neon gas, helium gas, or neon-helium mixed gas, and an inactive gas containing a small amount of xenon. When an electric discharge occurs within each cell by applying voltage to electrodes provided in respective directions inside the cells via external terminals connected thereto. 3. Summary of Invention [0007] In accordance with one embodiment described herein, there is disclosed: 1) a plasma display panel having barrier ribs formed on an upper plate and filled therein discharge gas containing at least xenon; said differential dielectric layer being disposed between the front substrate and each electrode to form stepwise difference in potential distribution when voltage applied. In addition thereto, it may also comprise of lower substrates including data driver ICs or scan drivers. 4. Description To describe more about this aspect briefly: as seen from fig 1(a) a panel structure is divided into three layers - upper plate and two bottom plates; the latter are composed by numerous parallel lines in horizontal direction, thus creating many discharge cells along each line on both substrates respectively. texts.full_text: a ferroelectric liquid-crystal spatial light modulator (FLC SLM) to control the transmittance of a telescope pupil to compensate for the effects of scintillation. Oxmitting laser pulses with intensities that are higher than those at which saturation occurs, and adjusting these by approximately 30% using an FLC SLM. The proposed correction technique will be demonstrated in a setup where we use the adaptive optics system of ESO's Very Large Telescope to imprint short wavelength light onto pupils. This approach could improve performance for shorter infrared laser pulses than those used today, enabling better detection and analysis at greater distances. To illustrate our method: 1) The intensity modulation range is determined by measuring how much the power in a beam can be reduced while still having enough remaining to produce images with sufficient photon counts. 2.) Using ESO's APEX/IMSIM system (Adaptive Optics System for Extremely Large Telescopes, Improved Modulator-Based Instruction Sets), we controlled an FLC SLM and varied the output intensity of a laser beam between 0% transmission to full power. The results were measured using detection optic assemblies that recorded photon counts in pixel-array detectors. 3.) In laboratory testing at different scintillation conditions, including fluctuations caused by wind or raindrops on Earth's surface and the variable distance from a binary star system acting as an extraterrestrial source of optical signals. We found significant improvements to image quality. This preliminary work indicates that FLC SLMs could substantially reduce atmospheric-induced images degradations due scintillation. texts.full_text: a pigment composition which shows excellent flowability and stability with time, when used for a gravure ink, a flexographic ink or an offset ink, and whose printed matter shows an excellent gloss, clearness, color strength and migrationfastness, and an ink composition using the same. Phthalocyanine type pigments have a clear color tone and high color strength and are excellent in various resistances such as weather fastness or chemical resistance, so that they are used in a wide range of uses. However, since they are hard to disperse in most cases, the value of a product is impaired in some uses. Particularly, when a phthalocyanine type pigment is used in a gravure ink, a flexographic ink or an offset ink, an obtained ink tends to have a high viscosity, since it is required to disperse the pigment in a vehicle for an ink such that the pigment has a relatively fine particle diameter of 0.1 xcexcm or less therein. Therefore, it is difficult to take out of the ink from an ink dispersion equipment and to transport it.

The Hoosiers' offense struggled to find holes in Boyd's pitching, managing only four hits throughout the game and being unable to capitalize on their few opportunities. KEY MOMENT: In what turned into a battle of wills between Slegers and Boyd, Oregon State managed its sole run off an RBI single by Cadyn Wilson during Indiana’s fifth inning. This was one of just two innings where both teams had base runners in scoring position, making the timely hit from Wilson crucial to his team's victory. THE IMPACT: With this loss, Indiana ends a remarkable season with 49 wins and exits their first College World Series appearance without winning an elimination game against Oregon State. texts.full_text: it. Then, if you wanted to get real sentimental and romantic, you would go into the hallways and sing. They had a perfect echo. Subwstlye acoustic properties in those narrow corridors! It was incredible how much richer your voice sounded there compared to out on an open street corner under that lonely lamp post. The community felt very close-knit back then, especially among young people who shared the same passions and dreams for making music. It's true that Doo-Wop captured something pure about those times—youth rebellion wrapped in tight vocal harmonies and a sense of togetherness amidst urban life. We had this camaraderie with other groups from different neighborhoods; it was like our own little world within the city projects where we could express ourselves freely through song. When I think back, that first record made under Bruce & The Q's name feels almost inevitable now—it came out of all those hallway harmonies and street-corner crooning. But while music remained central to my life from then on, producing allowed me a chance to nurture talent across different genres and help artists reach audiences they otherwise might not have reached. The journey after that was full of incredible experiences—working with amazing vocalists like Marion Butler whose range could touch the heavens; being part of tours in Africa

texts.full_text: harges plus interest thereon from date suit filed in 1973 to entry on judgment. The defendant appeals contending error was committed by granting of this motion as there were questions that should have been resolved only if the case had gone before jury; i.~e., whether he authorized plaintiff's services, or knew how much labor would cost and for what length time automobile might be stored at $5 per day charge. The evidence is in conflict on these points but we do not find it necessary to reach that question as there was no proof of the reasonable value required by Code Ann 20-1403 (Ga L, ."). The plaintiff's testimony shows he charged for labor at $5 per hour and storage charges were made while automobile remained in his garage. There is nothing showing what a fair price would be to fix this car or store it under the circumstances of repair involved here; nor that defendant knew how much labour might cost, although there was evidence they discussed costs on several occasions during repairs but no estimate as *462 to total amount due for parts plus labor charges. The trial court erred in directing a verdict and should have allowed case to go before jury unless plaintiff could show what would be fair compensation under the circumstances of this repair job, including storage fees charged while automobile was being repaired by him; or that defendant had agreed upon an express contract as contended *463 for parts plus labor charges. texts.full_text: ne graft materials have been regarded as adequate bone substitutes because of their availability without additional surgery \[[@B2]\]. In 1965, it was reported that the implantation of demineralized bone into extrabony sites led to the formation of bone ossicles \[[@B3]\]. In this process, known as osteoinduction, bone morphogenetic components stimulate undifferentiated mesenchymal cells to become osteoprogenitor cells and then differentiate them further along an osseous lineage \[@Ullrich2014]. The first demineralized freeze-dried allograft was introduced in the early $7\text{th}$ decade of last century \[[@B3]\]. Since that time, many studies have been conducted to evaluate its efficacy and safety as a bone graft material \[@Ullrich2014]. The aim for this study is: - to compare between the effect on osteogenesis in rabbit calvarial defects of two different types allogenic demineralized freeze dried grafts. texts.full_text: begged. The silence was overwhelming. Suddenly, the escalator stopped and my grandparents stepped off. They walked towards me with slow, deliberate steps. "We are here, child," my grandmother said, her voice echoing in the vast space. "Your mother is not here. She is lost, and so are you." I was shocked. "Lost? But why? Where did she go?" "She is in a place beyond our reach," my grandfather explained. "A place of darkness and despair. It is a place she chose over the light, and now she is forever trapped." "But why?" I whispered, my voice breaking. "Why did she leave?" My grandmother's eyes were filled with sadness. "She was cursed, my child. A curse that has plagued our family for generations. It drives those affected to self-destruction, and your mother could not escape its grasp." "A curse?" I repeated, my mind reeling. "But how can I break it? How can I save myself?" "You must find the source of the curse and end it," my grandfather said. "

texts.full_text: element of the tuple key in D2 is not in D1? For example, in this case, the key ('y','x2') in D2 should result in removing the key 'y' from D1 if it existed. A: Yes, you can achieve this by iterating through the keys of D2 and checking if the first element of the tuple is in D1. If not, you can remove that key from D1. Here's a Python code snippet to do this: ```python def remove_items(D1, D2): for key in list(D1.keys()): if not any(key == k[0] for k in D2.keys()): del D1[key] D1 = {'x':123, 'x1': 154, 'x2':184} D2 = {('x','x1'):50, ('x1','x2'):30, ('y','x2'):10} remove_items(D1, D2) print(D1) ``` In this code, we create a function `remove_items` that takes D1 and D2 as arguments. We iterate through the keys of D1 using `list(D1.keys())` to create a list of keys. For each key, we check if it equals the first element of any tuple key in D2 using a generator expression inside the `any` function. If it's not found, we delete that key from D1 using `del`. After running the code, the output will be: ``` {'x': 123, 'x1': 154, 'x2': 184} ``` As you can see, the key 'y' was not present in the original D1, so the output remains But if 'y' were in D1, it would have been removed due to the presence of the tuple key ('y','x2') in D2.

They extendnorth to south for morethan 600 km, and, in the centralpart of the range, their crest forms the border between China and Myanmar. They also separate and form parts of the watersheds of two of Southeast Asia's major rivers, the Salween and theMekong. texts.full_text: ds of others. In short, forget your needs. Focus first on the needs of others. I’m privileged to serve as Chairman and CEO of the Greater Philadelphia Senior Executive Group (GPSEG), which is among the region’s largest networking and professional development organizations. GPSEG’s 1,400-plus members – senior level executives, business owners and non-profit directors, among others – join to give something, not get something. Our first goal as business leaders is to make our networks and wealth of experience work for our fellow members. Yes, business development networking can establish or enhance your company’s brand. It can help you differentiate your business in a crowded and competitive market. But at a more basic level, it should also be viewed as a tool for strengthening relationships and establishing yourself as a trusted advisor to a growing band of people. Dale Carnegie’s off-cited quote – “You can make more friends in two months by becoming interested in other people than you can in two years by trying to get them interested in you.” – remains applicable at today’s coffee meetings and LinkedIn conversations. texts.full_text: i.e., resins with hardness values of about 60 Shore D and above, with relatively softer polymeric materials. For example, blends of hard ionomers with polyurethanes have been used to form intermediate layers and cover layers of golf balls. However, such blends generally have processing difficulties associated with their use in the production of golf balls due to the incompatibility of the components. In addition, golf balls produced from these incompatible mixtures will have inferior golf ball properties such as poor durability, cut resistance, and the like. texts.full_text: position when users walk or run on it. Therefore, to prevent the unwanted displacement or shifting of a belt, a belt guiding device is needed to keep a belt at a fixed position. FIG. 1 shows an embodiment according to prior art of a commonly used belt guiding design. As shown, an angled strip 11 is soldered on both side of the base of the support board 10. These two angled strips 11 press against both sides of the belt, so the object of belt guiding is achieved. However, the advantages of this embodiment including these angled strips are difficult to solder, and these angled strips can be bent, displaced or damaged by transporting or moving this machine. Therefore, FIG. 2 shows another embodiment of the prior art that has been on the market. This embodiment consists a larger guiding disk 14 on both sides of the front rollers 13; these two guiding dike 14 are connected by a long guiding rod 12, and the guiding rod 12 is connected to a support plate 15 and a base 16. This embodiment is structurally complicated and occupies a large space. Also, this embodiment has a problem that the guiding disk 14 and the guiding rod 12 are prone to be damaged or bent during transporting or moving this machine. The present invention has been accomplished under the circumstances described above. Another object of the present invention is to provide an improved belt guiding device for treadmill that can be easily fixed on the machine, and has a good belt guiding capability. texts.full_text: In this paper the effect of surface wettability on hepatocyte morphology and function was studied, using clean and octadecylsylane (ODS)-coated glass as a model for hydrophilic and hydrophobic surfaces, respectively. C3A cells--a hepatoblastoma cell line, and freshly obtained porcine hepatocytes were cultured for a short-time period of up to 4 days on the above substrata. Hepatocyte adhesive interactions were characterized monitoring the initial cell attachment, the overall cell morphology, the formation of focal adhesions, and actin filaments. Since hepatocytes showed a clear tendency for homotypic adhesion on ODS, specific E-cadherin staining was used to visualize the intercellular contacts by immunofluorescence microscopy. Additionally, functional assays were cconducted to evaluate the impact of surface wettability on cellular functions such as albumin secretion and urea production. The results showed that hepatocytes cultured on hydrophobic ODS-coated surfaces exhibited enhanced cell-cell interactions, with increased formation of focal adhesions and actin filaments, compared to those grown on clean glass substrates.
